title: bigo report
description: Machine-readable output of `bigo json`: per-function inferred asymptotic bounds, budget verdicts, unverifiability causes, and the trust surface. Stability policy: schema_version is semver, independent of bigo releases; within a major version changes are additive-only and no field is ever reinterpreted. Consumers must ignore unknown fields.

bigo_version.description: The bigo release that produced the document.

module.description: Go module path of the analyzed code; empty outside module mode.

functions.description: Every analyzed function and method, budgeted or not, sorted by package, file, line.

trusted.description: Every //bigo:cost and //bigo:ignore assertion found — the document's trust surface. Any entry may have influenced any verdict; audit these when auditing a document. v1 lists function-attached assertions; interface-method costs are a planned additive extension.

receiver.description: Method receiver type as written, e.g. "*Tree"; absent for plain functions.

causes.description: Why the time bound is top; present exactly when time.top is true.

budget.description: The //bigo:max declaration and its verdict, when declared.

trust.description: Raw //bigo:cost / //bigo:ignore directives declared on this function.

bound.description: An asymptotic bound: either top (unverifiable) or a canonical string plus the antichain of poly-log monomials.

top.description: True for the unverifiable bound; str and terms are absent.

terms.description: Antichain of monomials, sorted by canonical string. A monomial maps each size variable to its exponents; the empty object is O(1).

factor.description: One variable's exponents within a monomial: v^pow · (log v)^log. Omitted members are zero.

kind.description: Machine-readable cause category. Known kinds: call, defer, go, loop, irreducible, nobody. Open set — new engine work may add kinds; consumers must tolerate unknown values.

detail.description: Human-readable description. Presentation text — never dispatch on it.

file.description: Module-relative position of the blocker, when known.

raw.description: The directive as written, including any where-bindings.

bound.description: The normalized budget; absent when verdict is invalid.

verdict.description: Sound three-valued verdict, plus invalid for budgets that failed to normalize. Closed vocabulary — a new verdict would be a major schema change.

heap.description: Total-allocation upper bound; proves within only, never exceeds.

stack.description: Peak recursion depth; proves both within and exceeds.
